I time my blinking to the rhythm of the radiator's heartbeat, my signature bleeding into the wooden desk
The ink from the ledger has seeped through my cuticles, mapping new veins that match the building's floor plans
The vibration of the vending machine hums deep inside my molars
The vibration of the vending machine in my marrow and the ink of ghosts seeping into my skin
The 11:00 PM bell thuds, a low-frequency vibration traveling through the soles of my shoes
